questions.question: Does the synthesis pool unlike stages into a new quality score?
questions.evidence: Stage 1 is the general 64-task repeated sample; Stage 2 is a selected 16-task diagnostic holdout; Stage 3 changes concurrency. They remain separate evidence layers.

questions.question: Can the campaign claim Lexmount is generally more accurate?
questions.evidence: Stage 1 was 82/128 versus 80/128 with cluster CI [-9.375, +12.5]pp, and Stage 3 c32 was 40/64 versus 41/64.

questions.question: Can the campaign claim repeatable Lexmount benefits on some sites?
questions.answer: yes, within the selected diagnostic set
questions.evidence: Stage 2 retained 16/18 expected holdout directions and had four Lexmount-only tasks across all four repeats. The same data also had three all-four Local-only tasks.

questions.question: Does the failure evidence prove that IP or proxy caused the site effects?
questions.evidence: Captcha, denied-access, navigation, and control-chain signatures are associations. The experiment did not independently randomize IP, proxy, fingerprint, browser build, or provider host.

questions.question: Is c32 a verified operational point?
questions.evidence: Both c32 backends completed 64/64 trajectories and Judge records, with no session-create failure or guard; Lexmount observed active max 32 and residual 0.

questions.question: Is c64 a verified operational point?
questions.evidence: Quota attainability was shown with balanced 32/32, but the actual 28/36 allocation failed admission twice and no formal c64 Agent run was launched.

questions.question: Can runner resource ratios be described as end-to-end cloud savings?
questions.evidence: Local includes Chrome on the runner; Lexmount excludes remote browser hosts. The ratios describe runner displacement, not total provider cost.

questions.question: Are GPU differences part of the product conclusion?
questions.evidence: The model was remote and the host had unrelated GPU workloads; GPU metrics are context only.

questions.question: Were all formal campaign records complete?
questions.evidence: Across 12 formal arms, planned trajectories, completed trajectories, and valid Judge records were all 576.

questions.question: What should change the next decision?
questions.answer: cross-window c32 replication and task-shaped admission evidence
questions.evidence: These directly test the two remaining uncertainties: quality/resource stability over time and honest c64 availability.
